summary: Fixed the GitHub package/action entrypoint for `gen-pages` by making `.github/packages/gen-pages/entrypoint.sh` executable. The PR contains only a file mode change from `100644` to `100755`, with no discussion or code changes in the available slice.
what_changed: Changed `.github/packages/gen-pages/entrypoint.sh` file mode from non-executable `100644` to executable `100755`.
No source code, script text, decompilation logic, or generated output changed in the available diff.
smart_moves: Solved an entrypoint failure with the minimal required permission fix instead of changing script contents or workflow logic.
Kept the PR extremely small and low-risk, appropriate for a CI/package entrypoint issue.
decomp_lessons: Repository tooling scripts used as entrypoints must have executable permissions committed in git; otherwise package/action execution can fail even when the script contents are correct.
For infrastructure-only fixes, file mode changes can be the entire functional change and should be reviewed as such.
This PR does not provide decompilation matching, symbol, struct, or header lessons.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs touching `.github/packages/gen-pages` to understand the surrounding package/action setup.
Search for workflow failures or CI logs mentioning `gen-pages` entrypoint permissions if more context is needed.
